source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
title: Ensure security group changes are monitored

input_row.source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure security group changes are monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. Security groups are stateful packet filters that control ingress and egress traffic within a VPC. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established to detect changes to security groups.
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. Monitoring changes to security groups will help ensure that resources and services are not unintentionally exposed. Impact: This may require additional 'tuning' to eliminate false positives and filter out expected activity so that anomalies are easier to detect.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrail trails: aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log- group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: o aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> ▪ Ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: o aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> ▪ Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group- name> 3. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: "filterPattern": "{ ($.eventName = AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress) || ($.eventName = AuthorizeSecurityGroupEgress) || ($.eventName = RevokeSecurityGroupIngress) || ($.eventName = RevokeSecurityGroupEgress) || ($.eventName = CreateSecurityGroup) || ($.eventName = DeleteSecurityGroup) || ($.eventName = ModifySecurityGroupRules) }" 4. Note the <security-group-changes-metric> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 5. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <security-group-changes- metric> captured in step 4: aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query "MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<security-group-changes-metric>]" 6. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 7. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id>
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for security groups changes and uses the <trail-log-group-name> taken from audit step 1: aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> -- filter-name <security-group-changes-metric> --metric-transformations metricName=<security-group-changes- metric>,metricNamespace="CISBenchmark",metricValue=1 --filter-pattern "{ ($.eventName = AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress) || ($.eventName = AuthorizeSecurityGroupEgress) || ($.eventName = RevokeSecurityGroupIngress) || ($.eventName = RevokeSecurityGroupEgress) || ($.eventName = CreateSecurityGroup) || ($.eventName = DeleteSecurityGroup) || ($.eventName = ModifySecurityGroupRules) }" Note : You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 2. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note : You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note : Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 3. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note : You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms. 4. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name <security-group-changes- alarm> --metric-name <security-group-changes-metric> --statistic Sum -- period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --namespace "CISBenchmark" --alarm-actions <sns-topic-arn> References: 1. CCE-79195-4 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/receive-cloudtrail- log-files-from-multiple-regions.html 3. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudwatch-alarms- for-cloudtrail.html 4. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/SubscribeTopic.html 5. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/APIReference/API_ModifySecurity GroupRules.html Additional Information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored AWS has recently introduced a new API, ModifySecurityGroupRules, which modifies the rules of a security group.
input_row.impact: This may require additional 'tuning' to eliminate false positives and filter out expected activity so that anomalies are easier to detect.
input_row.references: 1. CCE-79195-4 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/receive-cloudtrail- log-files-from-multiple-regions.html 3. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudwatch-alarms- for-cloudtrail.html 4. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/SubscribeTopic.html 5. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/APIReference/API_ModifySecurity GroupRules.html Additional Information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored AWS has recently introduced a new API, ModifySecurityGroupRules, which modifies the rules of a security group.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control involves automated verification through AWS CloudTrail and CloudWatch, both of which have comprehensive API support via the AWS CLI and boto3 SDK. The audit process involves several steps using these services to ensure that security group changes are being monitored, which includes verifying the existence and configuration of CloudTrail trails, CloudWatch Logs, metric filters, and alarms. These are all achievable through AWS CLI commands and boto3 calls, aligning well with an automated approach.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudwatch_security_group_change_monitoring_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **Enumerate CloudTrail Trails:**
  - Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail')` with `describe_trails()` to list all CloudTrail trails and filter for those with `IsMultiRegionTrail` set to `True`.

- **Check Trail Status:**
  - Use `get_trail_status()` to ensure logging is active (`IsLogging` is `True`).

- **Verify Event Selectors:**
  - Use `get_event_selectors()` to confirm that management events are included (`IncludeManagementEvents` set to `True` and `ReadWriteType` set to `All`).

- **Verify CloudWatch Logs Configuration:**
  - For each trail, determine the associated CloudWatch log group by examining `CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn`.

- **Describe Metric Filters:**
  - Use `boto3.client('logs')` with `describe_metric_filters(logGroupName=<trail-log-group-name>)` to ensure the presence of a metric filter with the specified filter pattern for security group changes.

- **Check CloudWatch Alarms:**
  - Use `boto3.client('cloudwatch')` with `describe_alarms(MetricName=<security-group-changes-metric>)` to verify an alarm is set up with the proper configuration and associated with the correct SNS topic.

- **Verify SNS Subscriptions:**
  - Use `boto3.client('sns')` with `list_subscriptions_by_topic(TopicArn=<sns-topic-arn>)` to ensure there is at least one active subscription to the SNS topic specified by the alarm.

- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - **Pass:** A CloudTrail trail is active, capturing all management events, appropriately configured CloudWatch metric filters and alarms exist, and there is an active subscriber to the SNS notification.
  - **Fail:** Any of the configuration checks above are not met, signaling that monitoring of security group changes is not fully configured or active.

This automated check ensures that any changes to security groups (creation, modification, deletion) are detected promptly, contributing to robust security posture management.
step2_review:
### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: AGREE
- **Manual vs Automated Classification:** The classification as Automated is correct. The use of AWS CloudTrail, CloudWatch, and SNS, all of which have robust support through AWS APIs, boto3, and AWS CLI, justifies an automated approach.
- **AWS APIs/boto3/CLI:** The tasks outlined can indeed be accomplished using AWS APIs, boto3, and AWS CLI, making automation feasible and efficient.

### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: 8
- **Automation Details:** The automation steps are generally accurate. The use of `boto3.client('cloudtrail')` and `boto3.client('logs')` is appropriate for interacting with CloudTrail and CloudWatch Logs, respectively.
- **boto3 Methods/CLI Commands:** The methods such as `describe_trails()`, `get_trail_status()`, `get_event_selectors()`, `describe_metric_filters()`, and `describe_alarms()` are valid and correctly used.
- **Program Name:** The program name `aws_cloudwatch_security_group_change_monitoring_configured` is descriptive but could be more specific to include the aspect of "real-time monitoring" or "alert configuration".
- **Completeness:** The assessment covers the necessary steps for ensuring security group changes are monitored. However, it could include verification of the filter pattern used in metric filters to ensure it specifically targets security group changes.

### 3. IMPROVEMENTS:
- **Filter Pattern Verification:** Include a step to verify the specific filter pattern used in CloudWatch Logs to ensure it accurately captures security group changes. This could be done by checking the pattern against known API calls related to security group modifications (e.g., `AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress`, `RevokeSecurityGroupIngress`, etc.).
- **Program Name Suggestion:** Consider renaming the program to `aws_cloudwatch_security_group_change_alerts_configured` to emphasize the alerting aspect.
- **Alternative Methods:** Consider using AWS Config rules as an additional layer to ensure compliance with security group configurations and changes.

### 4. CONFIDENCE: HIGH
- The assessment is well-structured and leverages AWS services effectively. The technical details are mostly accurate, and the approach is sound.

### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: MODIFY
- **Modify:** Include verification of the specific CloudWatch Logs filter pattern to ensure it is correctly configured to detect security group changes. Additionally, consider refining the program name for clarity and specificity regarding alert configuration.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that all required audit and remediation steps for this control—verifying CloudTrail multi-region trails, CloudWatch log group associations, metric filters, and alarms for security group changes—can be fully accomplished using AWS APIs (boto3, AWS CLI). All relevant properties (trail status, log group ARNs, metric filter patterns, alarm configurations) are exposed via AWS SDKs, making automation not only feasible but preferable for accuracy and scalability.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
aws_cloudwatch_logs_security_group_change_monitoring_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS Resource/Service:** CloudTrail Trails, CloudWatch Log Groups, CloudWatch Metric Filters, CloudWatch Alarms
- **API/CLI Method:**
  - `boto3.client('cloudtrail').describe_trails()`
  - `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_trail_status()`
  - `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_event_selectors()`
  - `boto3.client('logs').describe_metric_filters(logGroupName=...)`
  - `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_alarms()`
- **Properties to Check:**
  - At least one CloudTrail trail with `IsMultiRegionTrail=True` and `IsLogging=True`
  - Trail has `CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn` set
  - Trail event selectors include management events (`IncludeManagementEvents=True`, `ReadWriteType='All'`)
  - CloudWatch Log Group has a metric filter with a pattern that matches security group changes (e.g., `"$.eventName=AuthorizeSecurityGroup* || $.eventName=RevokeSecurityGroup* || $.eventName=CreateSecurityGroup || $.eventName=DeleteSecurityGroup || $.eventName=UpdateSecurityGroup*"` per CIS guidance)
  - There is an active CloudWatch Alarm on the metric filter
- **Pass Criteria:**
  - All above properties are present and correctly configured for at least one multi-region CloudTrail trail
- **Fail Criteria:**
  - Any required property is missing, misconfigured, or the metric filter pattern does not match security group change events

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
Both AI opinions agreed on automation. The second opinion suggested a more descriptive program name and emphasized verifying the metric filter pattern. This has been incorporated into the final program name and automation details for completeness and precision.

**SOURCE:** CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
